task_completion.description: Verify that the sample order data has been loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Oracle Analytics Desktop interface is open, and the data panel on the left shows columns from the sample order data (including 'Profit').

task_completion.description: Verify that a histogram visualization has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bar chart or histogram visualization is visible in the main canvas area.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Profit' column is on the X-Axis and 'Count' or 'Frequency' is on the Y-Axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grammar/properties panel shows 'Profit' assigned to the X-Axis (or Category) and a count/frequency metric assigned to the Y-Axis (or Values). The chart axes should be labeled accordingly.

task_completion.description: Verify that the histogram is adjusted to approximately 20 bins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The histogram displays roughly 20 distinct bars (bins), or the properties panel explicitly shows the bin count configured to 20.

task_completion.description: Verify that the visualization title is set to 'Distribution of Order Profits'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Distribution of Order Profits' is clearly visible as the title of the visualization on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the workbook is saved as 'ProfitDistribution'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top application bar, a workspace tab, or a save dialog shows the workbook name as 'ProfitDistribution'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Oracle Analytics Desktop application interface being interacted with (menus, drag-and-drop, properties panel), not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the visualization is genuinely rendered by the software and not a mocked or downloaded image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visualization appears integrated within the Oracle Analytics Desktop workspace, with surrounding UI elements (menus, data panels, properties) consistent with the software.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the provided sample data rather than generating fake data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data panel shows columns consistent with the provided order data, and the histogram reflects a realistic distribution rather than a perfectly uniform or manually typed mock dataset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected workbook name is 'ProfitDistribution' and the visualization title must be exactly 'Distribution of Order Profits'. The histogram must use the 'Profit' column on the X-Axis with approximately 20 bins. The workbook is saved as a .dva file, typically visible in the application title bar or save dialog.
